name: ASIAN FISH FINGER WRAP

ingredients.raw: 1/2 head of red cabbage, thinly sliced
2 medium or 1 large carrot, cut into matchsticks
4 small white fish fillets (cod, halibut, pollock, haddock), cut into fish finger-sized rectangles
ingredients.item: small white fish fillets (cod, halibut, pollock, haddock)

ingredients.raw: salt and freshly ground black pepper
ingredients.item: salt and freshly ground black pepper

ingredients.raw: 1 avocado, peeled, stoned and thinly sliced
flaky sea salt and freshly ground black pepper, to taste
ingredients.item: flaky sea salt and freshly ground black pepper, to taste

directions: For the slaw, in a large bowl, whisk together the rice vinegar, soy sauce, honey, sesame oil, ginger and garlic to make the dressing.
Add the red cabbage, carrot, spring onions and radishes. Mix thoroughly and leave to marinate while you prepare and cook the fish.

Place the flour, beaten eggs and panko breadcrumbs in three separate shallow bowls. Season each fish finger with a pinch of salt and pepper, then place in the flour bowl and coat thoroughly, dusting off any excess. Do the same with the egg, then the same with the breadcrumbs. Place on a tray and repeat until all the fish fingers are coated.

Heat the oil in a large frying pan over a medium heat. When shimmering, add the fish fingers and fry until golden brown on the underside, about 3-4 minutes. Flip and repeat, frying until the other side is golden brown, another 3-4 minutes. Remove onto a plate lined with kitchen paper to drain off any excess oil.

To assemble each wrap: place a handful of slaw on a tortilla wrap, top with a quarter of the fish fingers and avocado slices, spoon over some Sriracha mayo, squeeze over a lime wedge, season to taste, then roll up and eat.

name: Awesome Slaw

ingredients.raw: 6 spring onions, white and light green parts only, thinly sliced
ingredients.item: spring onions, white and light green parts only

ingredients.raw: a small bunch of coriander leaves, roughly chopped
ingredients.item: a small bunch of coriander leaves

ingredients.raw: 2 tablespoons raw, unpasteurized apple cider vinegar (with mother)
salt and freshly ground black pepper, to taste
ingredients.item: salt and freshly ground black pepper, to taste

directions: Place all the slaw ingredients in a large mixing bowl and toss to combine.
To make the dressing, whisk together all the ingredients in a small bowl until fully emulsified, adding salt and pepper to taste.
Pour the dressing over the vegetables and mix thoroughly until glossy and fully coated in the dressing.

name: Baked Halibut with Borlotti Beans and Tomatoes

ingredients.raw: 2 x 400g tins of borlotti beans, drained and rinsed
ingredients.item: x 400g tins of borlotti beans, drained and rinsed

ingredients.raw: 2 garlic cloves, peeled and finely sliced
Sea salt and freshly ground black pepper
ingredients.item: Sea salt and freshly ground black pepper

ingredients.raw: Small handful of basil leaves, to serve (optional)
ingredients.item: Small handful of basil leaves, to serve (optional)

directions: Preheat the oven to 220°C/200°C fan/Gas 7.
Put the tomatoes and borlotti beans into a roasting tray and season with a little salt and pepper.

Season both sides of the fish with salt and pepper and place them on top of the beans and tomatoes. Sprinkle the fish with chilli flakes, then scatter the garlic, capers and rosemary over everything in the tray. Put the lemon slices on top, then pour over the wine and drizzle with olive oil.
Place the tray on the top shelf of the oven for 12-15 minutes, or until the fish is cooked through.
Meanwhile, place the broccoli in a large sauté pan with the water and butter. Season with salt and pepper and cook for 5 minutes, turning halfway through for even cooking.
When the halibut is cooked, sprinkle with the basil leaves and serve with the broccoli and crusty bread.

name: Banh Mi

ingredients.raw: 150g carrots, peeled and julienned into thin matchsticks or ribbons
about 300g daikon, peeled and julienned into thin matchsticks or ribbons
4 small French-style baguettes (or use Vietnamese baguettes if you can find them)
ingredients.notes: or use Vietnamese baguettes if you can find them

ingredients.raw: 2 spring onions, thinly sliced on an angle

directions: First, make the pickles. Place the carrots and daikon into a colander and scatter over a pinch of salt. Toss, leave to stand for a few minutes, then make the brine by stirring together the vinegar, sugar, 1 teaspoon of salt and 100ml of boiling water. Squeeze out the vegetables, then place in a clean jar or bowl and pour over the brine. Leave to stand while you make the sandwiches, or store in the fridge until ready to use - the longer you leave them, the more intense the flavour.

Place a wide frying pan over a medium heat. Add a splash of oil, then when shimmering hot, add the chops and cook until golden brown and cooked through, about 4 minutes on each side, depending on their thickness.

While the pork cooks, mix all the pork sauce ingredients together with a tablespoon of water. Remove the pork from the pan and cut into 1cm strips, then return to the pan along with the sauce and cook for 1 minute. Tip into a bowl while you make the sandwiches.

Split the baguettes and spread one side of each with mayo and one side with the pâté. To the mayo side, add a good squirt of Sriracha sauce, 3 cucumber slices, a spoonful of the pickles and some spring onions and coriander leaves. Add a quarter of the pork per baguette, and a little of its sauce, then tuck in.

name: BEEF BRISKET MADRAS
directions: Season the brisket while you heat a little oil in a large frying pan over a medium heat. Sear the brisket on all sides.
Transfer to the slow cooker with all the other ingredients and add 200ml of boiling water. Cook on high for 4 hours or low for 8 hours until the beef is soft.
Shred the meat using two forks in the pot, or, if you prefer, carefully remove the brisket from the sauce to a plate to do this, then return the beef to the slow cooker and stir to coat the meat.
Serve hot with rice or potatoes, if liked.

name: BEEF CHOW MEIN

ingredients.raw: 400g beef, such as rump or any cheap thin steak, sliced
ingredients.item: beef, such as rump or any cheap thin steak

ingredients.raw: small thumb-sized piece of fresh ginger, grated or chopped (I use frozen)
ingredients.item: small thumb-sized piece of fresh ginger

ingredients.raw: 200g dried egg or rice noodles, cooked according to packet instructions
ingredients.item: dried egg or rice noodles, cooked according to packet instructions

directions: Add all the ingredients, except the carrot and noodles, to the slow cooker. Cook on high for 3-4 hours or low for 6 hours.
Add the carrot for the last 30 minutes of cooking, then stir in your cooked noodles. Divide between bowls, top each with a sprinkling of sesame seeds and enjoy.

name: BEETROOT, CARROT & ONION BHAJI BURGERS

ingredients.raw: 2 carrots (100g), peeled and finely sliced into thin matchsticks
1 medium-sized beetroot, peeled and finely sliced into thin matchsticks
2 tablespoons finely chopped fresh coriander leaves
1 teaspoon finely chopped deseeded red or green chilli
salt and freshly ground black pepper
ingredients.item: salt and freshly ground black pepper

ingredients.raw: 8 mini naan, or cut up a large naan or 8 burger buns
ingredients.item: mini naan, or cut up a large naan or 8 burger buns

directions: Pour a tablespoon of oil into a small pan over a medium heat and add the mustard seeds. When they start to pop, add the cumin seeds and curry leaves. Cook for 1 minute, making sure the cumin doesn't burn. Remove from the heat and pan out into a large bowl.
Add the onion, carrots, beetroot, coriander, chilli, nigella seeds, lime juice and turmeric, along with a generous pinch of salt and pepper.

Stir together the flours and baking powder. Add to the bowl and then slowly add up to 175ml of cold water, stirring all the time, until everything is coated in a smooth batter, about the thickness of single cream.

Heat about 1cm of vegetable oil in a wide frying pan set over a medium heat. Line a plate with kitchen paper. Place the naan or burger buns in a low oven to warm up. Have ready all the pickles/chutneys and garnishes, plus garlic yoghurt, if using.

Use a large spoon to scoop a little of the burger mixture into the pan. Cook until golden, turning once, then lift out carefully using tongs. Cool a little, then taste and add more seasoning to the batter if needed.

When happy, use the spoon to make a bhaji burger about 8cm across and about 1.5cm thick. Cook two or three at a time until they turn a deep golden, 3-4 minutes. Keep an eye on how hot the oil is - if you use a pan thermometer 180°C/350°F is ideal. Then turn and repeat.
Remove from the pan and drain on the lined plate. Keep warm while you cook the rest.
Assemble the mini naan burgers quickly (two per person) and eat immediately.

name: Better Than Popeyes Chicken Sandwich

ingredients.raw: tsp (4g) serrano powder (or any spicy chili powder such as cayenne)
ingredients.item: tsp (4g) serrano powder (or any spicy chili powder such as cayenne)

ingredients.raw: ½ tsp (2g) freshly cracked black pepper
ingredients.item: ½ tsp (2g) freshly cracked black pepper

ingredients.raw: 8 cloves black garlic, puréed or finely chopped
ingredients.item: black garlic, puréed or finely chopped

ingredients.raw: Squeeze of fresh lemon juice, or to taste
ingredients.item: Squeeze of fresh lemon juice, or to taste

directions: Prepare the marinade. In a medium bowl, whisk together the buttermilk, salt, garlic powder, and serrano powder.
Add the chicken to the marinade, making sure the pieces are fully submerged. Cover with plastic wrap and refrigerate for at least 1 hour or overnight.

Prepare the flour mixture. In a large shallow baking dish, combine the all-purpose flour, salt, garlic powder, smoked paprika, oyster mushroom powder (if using), pepper, and cayenne (if you want it spicy). Whisk together until combined.

Fill a large deep cast-iron skillet or heavy-bottomed pot with about 2½ inches (6.25cm) oil. (Do not fill the pot more than three-quarters full.) Heat over medium heat until it reaches 350°F (180°C). Adjust the heat up and down to maintain that temperature.
While the oil is heating, drizzle 2 to 3 tablespoons (30-45ml) of the marinade into the flour mixture, and toss it around. (This gives you some extra-flaky bits on the chicken.)

name: Black Bean Tacos

ingredients.raw: a splash of olive or vegetable oil
ingredients.item: a splash of olive or vegetable oil

ingredients.raw: ½ red chilli, hot or mild, finely chopped
ingredients.item: ½ red chilli, hot or mild

ingredients.raw: 2 x 400g cans black beans in water (do not drain)
ingredients.item: x 400g cans black beans in water

ingredients.raw: 1 heaped teaspoon sweet (not hot) smoked paprika
freshly squeezed lime juice, to taste
ingredients.item: freshly squeezed lime juice, to taste

ingredients.raw: salt and freshly ground black pepper
ingredients.item: salt and freshly ground black pepper

ingredients.raw: 8 small corn or wheat tortillas, warmed
ingredients.item: small corn or wheat tortillas, warmed

ingredients.raw: optional sides/toppings: Guacamole, Slaw of your choice, Pink Pickled Onions, soured cream
ingredients.item: optional sides/toppings: Guacamole, Slaw of your choice, Pink Pickled Onions, soured cream

directions: Set a large pan over a medium heat. When hot, add a splash of oil and the onion. Cook, stirring, for about 8 minutes, until translucent and just beginning to brown.
Add the garlic and tomato purée along with a pinch of salt and cook, stirring, for 2-3 minutes, until neither the tomato purée nor garlic smells raw.
Turn down the heat to low and add the chopped tomatoes and chilli. Cook, stirring often, for up to 5 minutes, until the tomatoes begin to break down.

Add the beans and most of their liquid, along with the oregano, cocoa powder, sugar, cinnamon, paprika and some pepper. Bring up to a simmer, and leave to bubble gently for 10 minutes, keeping an eye on the liquid levels and adding a little dash of water if needed.
When ready, the beans should be saucy, but not soupy, so turn the heat up to finish if too wet. Remove from the heat. Taste and add more salt, if needed and a squeeze of lime juice.
Serve piled onto warm tortillas, with the feta, coriander leaves and your favourite extras/toppings alongside.

name: BROCCOLI WITH CRUMBS, CHILLI & ANCHOVY

ingredients.raw: 2 tablespoons olive oil, or more as needed
ingredients.item: olive oil, or more as needed

ingredients.raw: 2 large slices of day-old wholegrain sourdough bread, shredded into rough 1cm pieces
ingredients.item: large slices of day-old wholegrain sourdough bread

ingredients.raw: 1-3 teaspoons finely chopped red chilli, deseeded, or to taste
a generous pinch of fennel seeds (optional)
ingredients.item: a generous pinch of fennel seeds (optional)

ingredients.raw: 250g purple or tenderstem broccoli, sliced into 3-4cm lengths

directions: Heat the olive oil in your widest frying pan over a medium heat. Add the anchovies and let them melt in the pan, stirring a few times so they don't catch and burn. Add the shredded bread and cook, turning often, until golden and crisp. If the bread is browning too fast, reduce the heat. Add a splash more oil if the pan seems very dry. When the bread is almost done, add the chilli, garlic and fennel seeds, if using, and cook for 2 minutes, just until the garlic is fragrant and no longer smells raw.

Blanch the broccoli in a pan of boiling salted water for 2 minutes (we like it really crisp-cook it longer if you don't). Drain and let it steam briefly in the colander, as any water left on the broccoli will make the crumbs soggy.
Toss the broccoli and crumbs together, scraping up every last bit of the now deliciously flavoured oil. Serve hot or warm.

name: Buckwheat Pancakes

ingredients.raw: 1 tablespoon flavourless oil, plus extra for cooking
ingredients.item: flavourless oil, plus extra for cooking

ingredients.raw: fresh berries or defrosted frozen fruit
ingredients.item: fresh berries or defrosted frozen fruit

directions: Mix the dry ingredients together in a large bowl.
In a separate bowl, beat together the milk, egg, 1 tablespoon of oil, vanilla bean paste and lemon zest.
Pour the wet mixture into the dry bowl and stir to combine, making sure no lumps remain.
Place a large frying pan over a medium heat. Add as little oil as possible, just enough to barely coat the base.
When the pan and oil are hot, use a large spoon to portion out the batter into the pan, making thick pancakes around 8cm across.
Cook until small bubbles appear on top and the bottom is golden brown, then flip and cook the other side.
Set aside somewhere warm, like a low oven, or wrap in a clean tea towel while you cook the rest.
Eat immediately, while hot, with fruit, yoghurt and a little maple syrup.

name: Butter Chicken

ingredients.raw: 1 heaped tbsp curry paste of your choice
ingredients.item: heaped tbsp curry paste of your choice

ingredients.raw: 4 tbsp yogurt (or double cream if you're feeling more luxurious)
ingredients.notes: or double cream if you're feeling more luxurious

directions: Add all the ingredients, except the yogurt and serving suggestions, to the slow cooker. Cook on high for 4 hours or low for 7-8 hours.
Stir in the yogurt (or cream).
Top with fresh coriander and serve with two chapatis per person.

name: Cajun-Style Fish Wrap with Slaw

ingredients.raw: 4 small firm white fish fillets, such as cod, haddock or plaice
ingredients.item: small firm white fish fillets, such as cod, haddock or plaice

ingredients.raw: 4 big spoonfuls of slaw (either Classic Coleslaw or Grazka's Fennel Slaw)
ingredients.notes: either Classic Coleslaw or Grazka's Fennel Slaw

ingredients.raw: 1 large ripe avocado, peeled, stoned and thinly sliced
a few sprigs of dill, roughly chopped (optional)

directions: For the fish, mix the salt, spices, garlic and onion powders and herbs together in a bowl, then place on a plate.
Add 3 tablespoons of the butter to a large, heavy-based frying pan and gently melt, then pour it into a shallow bowl.
Dip each fish fillet in the melted butter, then dredge in the spice mix.

Place the same frying pan back over a medium-high heat and add the oil. Once it is shimmering, place the fillets in, one by one, laying them away from you to avoid being splashed by hot oil. Cook until browned, about 3-4 minutes. Turn the fish, add the remaining butter, breaking it up and placing it in various spots around the pan, and cook until the other side is brown and the fish flakes easily, about another 3-4 minutes. Remove the fillets onto a plate and squeeze a little lime juice over each one.

Assemble the wraps: place a layer of slaw on each wrap, followed by a fish fillet, then top with avocado slices, a drizzle of extra-virgin olive oil and a pinch of salt. Serve each with lime wedges on the side and dill scattered over, if liked.

name: Chicken Biryani

ingredients.raw: 3cm piece of fresh root ginger, peeled and grated
350g boneless chicken thighs, cut into 2cm cubes
Sea salt and freshly ground black pepper
ingredients.item: Sea salt and freshly ground black pepper

ingredients.raw: Small handful of fresh mint, finely chopped

directions: Place a large, non-stick saucepan over a medium-high heat and add the oil. When hot, add the onion and cook for 2-3 minutes. Add the carrot and cook for 2 minutes.
Stir in the garlic and ginger, then add the curry paste and cook for 1-2 minutes.
Add the chicken, stir well, then add the tomatoes, rice, stock and seasoning. Stir again and bring to a simmer. Cover and cook over a low heat for 10 minutes.

Meanwhile, prepare the minted yoghurt. Put the cumin into a small frying pan over a high heat and stir until roasted and aromatic. Transfer to a small bowl, add the yoghurt and mint, season with salt and pepper and mix well.
Roughly chop the spinach and coriander, then add them to the chicken pan together with the peas. Stir well and cook for a further 2-3 minutes.
Serve the curry in bowls with the minted yoghurt and poppadoms alongside.

name: Chicken Curry & Noodles

ingredients.raw: 5 garlic cloves, crushed or 1 tsp lazy garlic
tbsp cornflour mixed with 1 tbsp water (optional)
ingredients.item: tbsp cornflour mixed with 1 tbsp water (optional)

directions: Add all the ingredients, except the noodles, stock and cornflour, to the slow cooker. Cook on high for 4 hours or low for 6 hours.

Stir and add the noodles and stock. (Alternatively, you can skip this step and cook the noodles in a separate pan of boiling water on the hob according to the packet instructions.) If the sauce hasn't thickened, add the cornflour. Close the lid and cook on high for 15 minutes, stirring after 10 minutes. You may need to add a tiny bit more water as the noodles will absorb the curry sauce and stock quickly.

name: CHICKEN, CHORIZO & PRAWN JAMBALAYA

ingredients.raw: 1 red pepper, deseeded and thinly sliced
1 red chilli, deseeded and thinly sliced
a hefty pinch of saffron threads
ingredients.item: a hefty pinch of saffron threads

ingredients.raw: 12 raw king prawns, peeled and deveined
a large handful of fresh parsley, roughly chopped
ingredients.item: a large handful of fresh parsley

directions: Heat the oil in a large, heavy-based, deep pan with a lid (like a Dutch oven). When it is shimmering, add the chicken thighs, skin-side down. Season with a pinch of salt. Brown the meat for 5-6 minutes, and then flip over and brown for a further 2-3 minutes. Remove from the pan, roughly chop and put in a bowl.
Without wiping the pan, brown the chorizo for 5-6 minutes. Remove and add to the chicken bowl.

Again without wiping the pan, add the onion, red pepper and celery. Cook for 5 minutes, stirring often. Add the garlic, chilli, cayenne pepper, paprika, thyme, oregano and black pepper. Cook for another 3 minutes, stirring now and then.

Add the white wine and deglaze the bottom of the pan, scraping off any brown bits, then leave to bubble for 1-2 minutes. Add the chicken and chorizo back in, along with the rice, tomatoes, stock, saffron and bay leaves.

Cover and simmer for 20 minutes. Stir through the prawns, then cover again and cook for a further 8-10 minutes until the rice is tender and the prawns are pink and opaque. Double-check a piece of chicken to see if the meat is done and the juices run clear. If it is getting a bit dry and the rice has absorbed all the liquid before it has finished cooking, add a bit more hot stock.
Add salt and pepper to taste, stir through the parsley and serve immediately.

name: Chocolate Beetroot Cake

ingredients.raw: 150g good-quality dark chocolate (70% cocoa solids): 100g melted; 50g roughly chopped
ingredients.item: good-quality dark chocolate : 100g melted; 50g roughly chopped

ingredients.raw: 250g beetroot, peeled, cooked and roughly chopped
75g good-quality dark chocolate (70% cocoa solids), melted
2 tablespoons thick and creamy full-fat Greek-style live yoghurt
ingredients.item: thick and creamy full-fat Greek-style live yoghurt

directions: Heat the oven to 175°C/350°F/gas mark 4. Grease and line two 20cm springform cake tins.

Combine the melted chocolate, beetroot, oils, sugar, vanilla bean paste and salt in a food processor and blitz until smooth, scraping down the sides with a spatula once or twice. Add the egg yolks and blitz very briefly, just enough to mix.
In a separate bowl, beat the egg whites just until white and moussy.

Sift the cocoa, flour and baking powder into a large mixing bowl. Add the beetroot mixture and egg whites to the dry ingredients along with the chopped chocolate, and fold together until just combined.

Pour the batter into the prepared tins and bake in the oven for 30 minutes, or until a skewer inserted into the middle comes out clean. Leave to cool in the tins for 15 minutes before placing on a rack to cool completely.
When cooled, sandwich the cakes together with the fruit compote.

To make the ganache, beat all the ingredients together until completely smooth and uniform in colour. Working quickly, before it firms up, spread over the top of the cake. Set aside at least until the ganache is set, before serving.

name: CRAB, WATERCRESS & MANGO SALAD

ingredients.raw: 80g fine green beans, cut in half
ingredients.item: fine green beans, cut in half

ingredients.raw: 1/2 large ripe mango, cut into 2cm chunks
salt and freshly ground black pepper, to taste
ingredients.item: salt and freshly ground black pepper, to taste

ingredients.raw: extra virgin olive oil, to serve
ingredients.item: extra virgin olive oil, to serve

ingredients.raw: 1 lime: 1/2 juiced; 1/2 cut into wedges, to serve
ingredients.item: lime: 1/2 juiced; 1/2 cut into wedges, to serve

ingredients.raw: salt and freshly ground black pepper, to taste
ingredients.item: salt and freshly ground black pepper, to taste

directions: Cook the green beans in a pan of boiling water for 2 minutes. Immediately drain, transfer to a bowl of ice-cold water to cool, then drain again. Set aside.
To make the dressing, combine the crème fraîche, mustard, lime juice, dill and chives together in a small bowl. Add salt and pepper to taste.

In a large mixing bowl, combine the cooked beans, watercress, spring onions, crab meat and mango. Add most of the dressing, saving a little bit to drizzle over at the end. Mix until well combined. Taste for seasoning and acidity, adding salt, pepper and lime juice, to your taste.
Divide between bowls, spoon over the remaining dressing and drizzle over some extra virgin olive oil. Serve with lime wedges.

name: Crispy Chicken, Quinoa and Cauliflower 'Couscous' with Charred Corn

ingredients.raw: 500g boneless chicken thighs, cut into strips 1cm thick
Sea salt and freshly ground black pepper
ingredients.item: Sea salt and freshly ground black pepper

ingredients.raw: 150g roasted red peppers, from a jar, thickly sliced
ingredients.item: roasted red peppers, from a jar, thickly sliced

ingredients.raw: 1 x 400g tin of kidney beans, drained and rinsed
ingredients.item: x 400g tin of kidney beans, drained and rinsed

ingredients.raw: 2 large handfuls of fresh coriander, roughly chopped

directions: Put the chicken, chipotle paste and oregano into a bowl. Season with salt and pepper and mix well.
For the 'couscous', remove the corn kernels from the cobs by standing each cob upright and running a sharp knife down the sides.
Place a large frying pan over a high heat and add the olive oil. When hot, fry the corn until lightly charred.
Add the onion and cook for 1-2 minutes. Add the cauliflower and cook for a further 2-3 minutes.
Remove the pan from the heat and stir in the broccoli, quinoa, red pepper, kidney beans, coriander and shredded lettuce. Transfer to a bowl and put to one side.

Place a shallow sauté pan over a high heat and add a 1cm depth of oil. Combine the flour and cornflour in a bowl, then lightly coat each piece of chicken in the mixture. Add to the hot oil in batches and cook until crisp outside with no sign of pink inside. When ready, drain on kitchen paper.

Meanwhile, whisk together the dressing ingredients and season with salt and pepper. Pour over the 'couscous' and mix well. Spoon onto plates, top with the crispy fried chicken and serve with wedges of lime.

name: Crispy Sweet Potato Fritters

ingredients.raw: 1 tablespoon black sesame seeds, toasted in a hot dry pan then roughly crushed
ingredients.item: black sesame seeds, toasted in a hot dry pan then roughly crushed

ingredients.raw: 3 spring onions, finely chopped, reserving a handful to garnish
splash of soy sauce/tamari, or to taste
ingredients.item: splash of soy sauce/tamari, or to taste

directions: Simmer the potato in a large pan of water for about 15 minutes until tender. Drain and steam dry for a minute, then mash thoroughly, removing any fibrous lumps.
Mix half of the crushed sesame seeds into the mash, along with the miso, soy sauce/tamari and spring onions. Leave to cool, then chill in the fridge for 30 minutes to firm up.
When ready to cook, mix the remaining sesame seeds with the breadcrumbs in a small bowl. Place the beaten eggs in another bowl and a few tablespoons of flour on a plate. Heat the oven to a low heat.
Mix together the sauce ingredients and, separately, the mayo ingredients.

Pour 5mm of oil into a frying pan set over a medium heat. Scoop up a small ball of the sweet potato mixture and shape into a little patty. It will be quite soft and sticky, so smaller fritters will hold together better. Dip it into the flour, then the egg, coating thoroughly, then finally roll it in the sesame breadcrumbs. Place it carefully into the hot oil and cook for 3-4 minutes, until the bottom is a deep golden brown, crisp and firm. Flip and cook the other side.

Work in batches, wiping out the pan and adding fresh oil with each batch, otherwise the pan will fill with loose, burning breadcrumbs. Add more flour to the plate, as needed. Keep the cooked fritters warm in the oven. Serve hot, garnished with the reserved spring onions and the sauces for drizzling or dipping.

name: Everyday Dhal

ingredients.raw: 200g yellow split peas, picked over and rinsed
ingredients.item: yellow split peas, picked over and rinsed

ingredients.raw: 3 cloves of garlic, crushed or finely grated
3cm piece of ginger, peeled and finely grated
1-3 teaspoons finely chopped red chilli, to taste
a squeeze of lime or lemon juice
ingredients.item: a squeeze of lime or lemon juice

ingredients.raw: salt and freshly ground black pepper
ingredients.item: salt and freshly ground black pepper

ingredients.raw: a handful of fresh coriander, to garnish
ingredients.item: a handful of fresh coriander, to garnish

directions: Place the yellow split peas and 1 litre of water in a large deep pan with a lid and bring up to a fast boil. Use a large spoon to skim off and discard any scum that rises to the surface. Turn the heat down to a simmer, cover and cook for 30 minutes.
Add the red lentils to the pan along with another 1 litre of water, cover and cook for a further 20 minutes, or until the red lentils are soft and collapsing into the broth.

Meanwhile, make the temper mixture. Add 1 tablespoon of the oil to a frying pan set over a medium heat. Add the onion and cook for 12-15 minutes, stirring often until caramelized and brown. Add the garlic and ginger and cook for 2-3 minutes, then add the remaining oil and the mustard seeds. When they begin to pop, add the rest of the spices, the fresh chilli and dried chilli flakes and a generous pinch of black pepper. Cook until the spices are fragrant but not scorched.

When the split peas are soft and the lentils have collapsed, remove the dhal pan from the heat. Pour in the hot temper mixture, including the oil, and stir. Add some salt, then taste and add more as needed (you can also add more hot water, if you like a soupier dhal). Just before serving, squeeze in a little fresh lemon or lime juice and stir again.
Serve garnished with a little fresh coriander and eat hot, with parathas, plain yoghurt or raita and spicy chutney or pickles.

name: FALAFEL & HARISSA WRAPS

ingredients.raw: 400g canned chickpeas, very well drained and rinsed
ingredients.item: canned chickpeas, very well drained and rinsed

ingredients.raw: dusting of freshly ground black pepper
ingredients.item: dusting of freshly ground black pepper

ingredients.raw: crisp lettuce leaves, slices of cucumber, pickled green chillies
ingredients.item: crisp lettuce leaves, slices of cucumber, pickled green chillies

ingredients.raw: optional extras: crumbled feta cheese or strips of fried halloumi, Tzatziki (see page 194), Pickled Radishes (see page 185), Pink Pickled Onions (see page 185) or finely sliced spring onions
ingredients.item: optional extras: crumbled feta cheese or strips of fried halloumi, Tzatziki (see page 194), Pickled Radishes (see page 185), Pink Pickled Onions (see page 185) or finely sliced spring onions

directions: For the falafel, place the chickpeas, onion, garlic, salt, cumin, coriander, cayenne pepper, cinnamon, pepper and lemon juice in a food processor and pulse until you have a thick, rough paste.
Tip this mixture into a bowl, then add the baking powder and both flours and stir well to combine.
The mixture should be scoop-able, but not sloppy. If it seems wet, add another tablespoon (or two) of chickpea flour.
Set a large frying pan over a medium heat and add about 12cm of olive oil.
When shimmering hot, add a little of the falafel mixture to the pan and cook for 2 minutes, turning, then cool and taste.
Add salt or any more of the other flavourings, to taste.
When ready to cook, use a dessertspoon to portion 3-4 falafel into the pan and flatten them into patties, each about 8cm across.
Cook gently for about 4 minutes, or until deep golden brown - turn the heat down if they cook too fast.
Gently flip each one over and cook until both sides are crisp, about 3-4 minutes more.
Remove from the pan and drain on a plate lined with kitchen paper while you cook the rest in the same way, adding more oil as needed (you'll make about 12 falafel in total).
To assemble, lay out the flatbreads/wraps and place three falafel on each one, leaving some room at the bottom to turn up the wrap later.
Drizzle over some harissa and tahini sauce, making sure it hits each falafel.
Arrange the lettuce, cucumber and pickled chillies next to the falafel, and add any extra fillings, if using.
Turn the bottom of each wrap up to enclose, then roll the sides inwards.
Scoff immediately.

name: Flour Tortillas

ingredients.raw: 2 cups (300g) unbleached all-purpose flour, plus more for dusting
ingredients.item: unbleached all-purpose flour, plus more for dusting

directions: In a large bowl, whisk together the all-purpose flour, salt, and baking powder until thoroughly combined.
While mixing with a spoon, pour in the warm water, immediately followed by the oil. Mix until it comes together as a ball of dough.
Turn the dough onto an unfloured surface, and knead for about 3 minutes, or until it comes together as a smooth ball of dough.
Cover with a damp towel, and let rest at room temperature for 30 minutes.
Divide the dough into 6 even pieces for burrito-sized tortillas or up to 12 pieces for taco-sized. Roll each piece into a ball.
On a lightly floured surface, gently roll each ball of dough to form a 10- to 12-inch (25-30.5-cm) circle.

Preheat a 12-inch (20.5-cm) cast-iron skillet over medium-high heat until very hot. Working 1 tortilla at a time, gently lay the tortilla in the skillet and cook for 15 to 30 seconds, or until the bottom becomes lightly charred. Flip and cook until lightly charred on the other side.
Remove from the pan and place between a folded towel until ready to serve. Repeat to cook the remaining tortillas. Enjoy!

name: Fried Fish Tacos

ingredients.raw: 1 batch beer-battered fish (exclude the sauce gribiche)
½ head green cabbage, very thinly sliced
ingredients.item: ½ head green cabbage, very thinly sliced

ingredients.raw: ½ large red onion, thinly sliced
Zest and juice of 2 meyer or regular lemons
ingredients.item: Zest and juice of 2 meyer or regular lemons

ingredients.raw: Kosher salt and freshly cracked black pepper, to taste
ingredients.item: Kosher salt and freshly cracked black pepper, to taste

directions: Make the crema. In a small bowl, whisk together the sour cream, mayonnaise, hot sauce, smoked paprika, chili oil, vinegar, and garlic. Season to taste generously with salt. Set aside until ready to serve.

Make the slaw. In a medium bowl, toss together the cabbage, onion, carrots, and cilantro until incorporated. Add the zest and juice of the lemons and the olive oil, and season to taste with salt and pepper. Toss again to thoroughly distribute, and set aside for at least 5 minutes or until ready to serve.

Heat a medium skillet over medium-high heat. Once hot, working 1 at a time, very lightly char the tortillas on both sides. If the pan is hot enough, this should take 20 to 25 seconds per side. Turn the heat down if they're charring too quickly.

Assemble each taco by stacking 2 tortillas per taco. Top with the beer-battered fish, the slaw, and a generous drizzle of the crema. Garnish with cilantro leaves and pickled jalapeños (if using). Add a squeeze of lime juice (if using). Fold as a taco, and enjoy!

name: Fruit & Flaxseed Muffins

ingredients.raw: 75ml very mild-tasting extra virgin olive oil
ingredients.item: very mild-tasting extra virgin olive oil

ingredients.raw: 1 teaspoon raw, unpasteurized apple cider vinegar

directions: Preheat the oven to 190°C/375°F/gas mark 5. Line a muffin tin with 10 paper cases.
Mix the flaxseeds with the water in a small bowl and leave to soak for 5 minutes.
Whisk together the oil, milk, vinegar and lemon zest.
Sift the flour, baking powder and bicarbonate of soda together in a large bowl. Add the salt and sugar and mix thoroughly. Add the ground almonds and frozen berries and mix again.
Add the oil and flaxseed mixtures to the bowl and stir until just combined.
Divide the mixture between the paper cases and sprinkle with the demerara sugar, if using.
Bake in the oven for 30-35 minutes. When done, the tops should be light golden and firm, and a skewer inserted into the middle should come out clean.
Leave to cool in the tin for 5-10 minutes, then turn out and cool on a rack.

name: GARLICKY PRAWNS

ingredients.raw: 1 teaspoon caper brine (optional, but adds a lovely tang)
ingredients.notes: optional, but adds a lovely tang

ingredients.raw: zest and juice of 1 unwaxed lemon
ingredients.item: zest and juice of 1 unwaxed lemon

ingredients.raw: 750g raw king prawns, peeled and deveined
salt and freshly ground black pepper
ingredients.item: salt and freshly ground black pepper

directions: If you want to make it easier to turn the prawns, thread them onto wooden/bamboo skewers, which have been soaked in water.
So simple, so tasty. This works best on a barbecue because of the smoky flavor, but you can use this butter mix to cook the prawns in the oven or in a pan, if preferred.
Heat the barbecue or grill to medium-high.

Heat the butter with the oil in a small saucepan over a medium heat until the butter melts, then add the garlic, shallot, oregano, red pepper flakes, cayenne pepper, caper brine, if using, and lemon juice.
Cook for 1 minute until fragrant, taste and adjust the seasoning, taste again and then remove from the heat.
If it starts to solidify before you start cooking your prawns, heat gently until liquid again.
Lay out the prawns on the barbecue, or in a grill pan if grilling, then grill until golden and pink, brushing often with the garlic butter, and turning them once.
They will need 2-3 minutes per side.
Transfer to a plate, brush again with any remaining garlic butter and scatter over the lemon zest and parsley.

name: Grilled Mackerel with Orange Gremolata

ingredients.raw: 2 garlic cloves, peeled and finely chopped
Zest and juice of 1 small orange
ingredients.item: Zest and juice of 1 small orange

ingredients.raw: 2 tbsp roughly chopped flat leaf parsley
Sea salt and freshly ground black pepper
ingredients.item: Sea salt and freshly ground black pepper

directions: Preheat the grill to medium-high. Line the grill pan with foil.
To make the orange gremolata dressing, put all the ingredients for it into a bowl and season with salt and pepper. Mix well, then put to one side.

Using a sharp knife, score the skin on the mackerel fillets, then place them on the prepared grill pan, skin side down. Drizzle olive oil over each of the fillets, add a squeeze of orange juice and scatter the rosemary sprigs on top.
Place the pan under the grill and cook the fish for 1-2 minutes before turning over and cooking for another 4-5 minutes, or until the skin is crisp and the flesh is opaque.
Transfer the fish to a platter and spoon over the gremolata dressing, before serving with a large green salad and lots of warm crusty bread.

name: Harissa Chicken Kebabs with Herby Yoghurt & Pomegranate

ingredients.raw: a splash of olive oil, if needed
ingredients.item: a splash of olive oil, if needed

ingredients.raw: zest and juice of ½ unwaxed lemon
ingredients.item: zest and juice of ½ unwaxed lemon

ingredients.raw: a small bunch of fresh basil leaves, finely chopped
ingredients.item: a small bunch of fresh basil leaves

ingredients.raw: a small bunch of fresh mint leaves, finely chopped
ingredients.item: a small bunch of fresh mint leaves

ingredients.raw: salt and freshly ground black pepper
ingredients.item: salt and freshly ground black pepper

ingredients.raw: 4 flatbreads (khobez or naan breads work really well), warmed
ingredients.notes: khobez or naan breads work really well

ingredients.raw: a handful of toasted pine nuts
ingredients.item: a handful of toasted pine nuts

directions: Cut the chicken into 3cm cubes, place in a large mixing bowl and rub with the harissa, cumin, salt and pepper. Add a splash of olive oil if the mix is a bit sticky. Cover and leave to marinate in the fridge for at least an hour (4-6 hours would be ideal).
Put the yoghurt, grated garlic, lemon zest and juice, basil and mint into a bowl and mix well until everything is combined. Season with a little salt and pepper. Cover and set aside.

Thread the chicken pieces evenly onto the skewers. Heat the vegetable oil in a griddle pan over a medium-high heat. When hot, place the skewers in the pan and griddle until a golden char appears on the bottom, about 5 minutes. Resist the temptation to keep turning them. Turn over with some tongs and griddle on the other side until the meat is fully cooked. Transfer to a plate while you grill the lemons.

Cut the lemons in half and remove any visible seeds with the tip of your knife. In the same pan as you cooked the chicken, place the lemons, cut-side down, and cook for 3-4 minutes, or until a lovely brown crust appears on the cut sides. Set aside.

To assemble the wraps, place a flatbread in the palm of your hand and use it to pull the chicken off one skewer. Slather on some yoghurt, sprinkle over some pine nuts and pomegranate seeds, and serve each with a charred lemon half, for squeezing.

name: Honey Ribs
directions: Place all the ingredients, except the sesame seeds, into the slow cooker with around 750ml of boiling water or chicken stock to cover the ribs. Cook on high for 4 hours or low for 8 hours until the meat is completely tender.

Remove the ribs, place them on a baking tray and cut them up into individual ribs. Transfer the juices to a saucepan and boil until reduced, thick and glossy. Drizzle the sauce over the ribs, then pop under a hot grill until they crisp up. Serve hot with sesame seeds sprinkled on top. You could also add some sliced spring onion, if you like.

name: KALE, PAK CHOI & BROCCOLI WITH GARLIC FRIED RICE

ingredients.raw: 1 large head garlic, cloves peeled and finely chopped
ingredients.item: large head garlic, cloves peeled and finely chopped

ingredients.raw: 8-10 spring onions, light green and white parts thinly sliced, green parts thinly sliced and reserved to serve
ingredients.item: spring onions, light green and white parts thinly sliced, green parts thinly sliced and reserved to serve

ingredients.raw: 100g tenderstem broccoli, sliced into even-sized batons
1 pak choi, thick stems removed, sliced the same size as the broccoli

directions: To make the crispy garlic, lightly season the chopped garlic with salt. In a small pan, heat the oil over a medium heat. Add the garlic and fry until very slightly browned and crispy, 2-3 minutes. The garlic should be gently bubbling, not spitting-reduce the heat if needed. Keep watch as the garlic can easily burn and turn bitter. Once the garlic is crispy, drain immediately, reserving the oil. Transfer the garlic to a plate lined with kitchen paper, to cool.

Heat 1 tablespoon of the garlic oil in a large wok or frying pan over a high heat. When smoking hot, add the spring onions, kale, broccoli and pak choi. Season lightly with salt. Stir fry, tossing constantly, until softened and fragrant, 2-3 minutes. Add more garlic oil as needed to prevent sticking. Transfer to a plate.

Add another tablespoon of garlic oil to the same pan. Add the rice and break up any clumps with a wooden spoon. Stir fry until the rice is broken up and softened, 1-2 minutes. Return the vegetables to the pan and add the soy sauce/tamari, dark soy sauce/tamari and sugar. Toss to combine and fry for a further minute.
Divide between 4 bowls and top generously with crispy garlic and sliced spring onion greens. Serve with a drizzle of sesame oil, lime wedges and Sriracha.

name: KIDNEY BEAN, CORN & CHIPOTLE SOUP

ingredients.raw: 400g canned kidney beans, in water, drained but not rinsed
ingredients.item: canned kidney beans, in water, drained but not rinsed

ingredients.raw: 400g canned black beans, in water, drained but not rinsed
ingredients.item: canned black beans, in water, drained but not rinsed

ingredients.raw: 100g courgette, yellow courgette or summer squash, chopped into small 1cm cubes
ingredients.item: courgette, yellow courgette or summer squash

directions: Heat the oil in a deep, wide pan with a lid over a medium heat. When hot, add the onion and cook for about 10 minutes, stirring often, until soft but not browned.
Add the garlic, cumin, paprika and chipotle paste and cook, stirring all the time, for 1 minute, until it all starts to smell delicious and the garlic no longer smells pungent and raw.
Add the boiling water and both cans of beans. Bring back to a simmer and cook for 5 minutes.
Finally, add the courgette or squash and the sweetcorn and bring back to a simmer again.
Remove from the heat, divide between bowls and top with the crumbled feta, spring onion and a little squeeze of lime juice. Eat immediately.

name: Lentil Burgers

ingredients.raw: 2 garlic cloves, peeled and finely chopped
2 green jalapeño chillies, deseeded and finely chopped
1 x 400g tin of chickpeas, drained and rinsed
ingredients.item: x 400g tin of chickpeas, drained and rinsed

ingredients.raw: 1 x 250g packet of cooked Puy lentils
ingredients.item: x 250g packet of cooked Puy lentils

ingredients.raw: Sea salt and freshly ground black pepper
ingredients.item: Sea salt and freshly ground black pepper

ingredients.raw: 2 tsp chipotle peppers in adobo sauce
1 ripe avocado, peeled, stoned and thinly sliced

directions: Preheat the oven to 200°C/180°C fan/Gas 6.

Place a non-stick frying pan over a high heat and add half the olive oil. When hot, add the onion and cook for 2-3 minutes, or until softened. Add the garlic, chillies and red pepper and cook for a further 2-3 minutes. Stir in the cumin, paprika and a big pinch of salt, then remove from the heat.
Put the chickpeas into a food processor and tip in the onion mixture. Pulse just until the mixture combines - you want to retain some texture. Transfer this mixture to a large bowl.

Add the lentils, breadcrumbs and beaten egg to the bowl, season with salt and pepper, and mix well with clean hands. Divide the mixture into 4 equal-sized burgers. Dust both sides with the flour, brushing off any excess.

Place a large, non-stick frying pan over a high heat and add the remaining 2 tablespoons of olive oil. When hot, add the burgers and cook for 2 minutes on each side. Transfer them to a baking tray and top each one with a slice of cheese. Place in the oven for 5 minutes. When cooked, remove from the oven and switch the grill on.
Meanwhile, mix together the mayonnaise, chipotle sauce and mustard.

Place the hamburger buns, cut side up, under the grill for 1-2 minutes, or until lightly toasted. Spread both halves with some of the mayonnaise mixture, then place the lettuce leaves on each bottom half, followed by a burger. Top with the gherkins, avocado, lettuce leaves and bun lids, and serve straight away.

name: Maine-Style Lobster Rolls

ingredients.raw: Zest and juice of 1 lemon, plus more zest to garnish
ingredients.item: Zest and juice of 1 lemon, plus more zest to garnish

ingredients.raw: 1 tbsp (4g) finely sliced chives, plus more to garnish
ingredients.item: finely sliced chives, plus more to garnish

ingredients.raw: Kosher salt and freshly cracked black pepper, to taste
ingredients.item: Kosher salt and freshly cracked black pepper, to taste

directions: Crack the lobster shells and remove the meat (in one piece, as possible). Set on a paper towel to drain slightly.

In a medium bowl, add the mayonnaise, lemon zest and juice, chives, dill, tarragon, and salt and pepper to taste. Whisk together until thoroughly combined. Leave the lobster whole, if desired, or cut it into large bite-sized pieces. Once you've finally made that decision, add the lobster to the mayonnaise mixture, and toss to coat evenly.

With a sharp knife, split the buns and carefully open them. Spread the softened butter on the inside of the buns. Place them butter-side down in a cold nonstick pan, and place over medium heat. Toast for 2 minutes, or until golden brown.
Stuff the buns with the lobster salad. Top with additional sliced chives, pepper, and lemon zest, and enjoy!

name: MISO-CRUSTED SALMON WITH BLACK R RICE & CUCUMBER PICKLE

ingredients.raw: 1 teaspoon mirin (or a pinch of sugar)
1 teaspoon soy sauce/tamari (use GF if needed), plus extra to serve
ingredients.item: soy sauce/tamari , plus extra to serve

ingredients.raw: 4 salmon fillets (about 120g each, try to use wild rather than farmed salmon)
ingredients.notes: about 120g each, try to use wild rather than farmed salmon

ingredients.raw: a pinch of black or white sesame seeds, or a mixture, to serve
ingredients.item: a pinch of black or white sesame seeds, or a mixture, to serve

ingredients.raw: 125g cucumber, sliced into long thin ribbons
a really generous pinch of sugar
ingredients.item: a really generous pinch of sugar

ingredients.raw: a pinch of black or white sesame seeds, or a mixture
ingredients.item: a pinch of black or white sesame seeds, or a mixture

ingredients.raw: 1/2 teaspoon rice vinegar or raw, unpasteurized apple cider vinegar (with mother)
ingredients.item: rice vinegar or raw, unpasteurized apple cider vinegar

ingredients.raw: 1 teaspoon very finely grated fresh ginger

directions: Place the rice in a pan with a lid (use a pan that won't stain), cover with cold water, and bring to a simmer over a medium heat. Cook until tender, up to 35 minutes. Drain any excess water, fluff up with a fork and cover with the lid until ready to serve.
Meanwhile, thoroughly mix the mirin, miso paste and soy sauce/tamari together, then rub the mixture all over the salmon fillets. Set aside.
Mix all the cucumber pickle ingredients together in a small bowl, and set aside.
Once the rice is cooked, heat the grill to medium.
Place the beans in a pan of boiling water, bring back to the boil and simmer for a couple of minutes. Drain and keep warm.

When the grill is hot, grill the salmon, skin-side up, for 2 minutes. Cook until dark but not scorched (the marinade can easily burn), then turn the salmon and cook the other side for 3-4 minutes, or until the salmon is just cooked through and beginning to flake. Don't allow it to overcook and fall apart.
Serve in wide bowls, with the rice on the bottom, the edamame beans and salmon on top, and the cucumber pickle alongside. Finish with a drizzle of toasted sesame oil and extra soy sauce on the table.

name: Moules Marinière with Wild Garlic Toasts

ingredients.raw: 2 banana shallots, peeled and finely diced
1 large garlic clove, peeled and finely chopped
Large handful of wild garlic leaves, finely chopped
ingredients.item: Large handful of wild garlic leaves

ingredients.raw: Handful of wild garlic, roughly chopped
4-6 slices of baguette, sliced on the diagonal
Sea salt and freshly ground black pepper
ingredients.item: Sea salt and freshly ground black pepper

directions: Preheat the grill to medium-high.
Wash and debeard the mussels, then drain in a colander.

Place a large casserole dish that has a tight-fitting lid over a medium heat and add the oil. When hot, add the shallots and cook for 2-3 minutes, or until softened. Stir in the garlic and cook for 1 minute, then add the wine. Simmer until the wine reduces by half.

Meanwhile, to make the wild garlic butter for the toasts, put the 150g butter into a small food processor with the roughly chopped wild garlic and a little salt and pepper. Blend until well combined. Set aside.
Add the cream and finely chopped wild garlic to the wine mixture, increase the heat to high and let it reduce by half.

Meanwhile, put the baguette slices on a baking tray and place under the grill for 2 minutes, or until golden and toasted on one side. Remove from the grill, flip each slice over, then spread thickly with the wild garlic butter. Using a fine grater, grate the Parmesan directly over each slice of baguette until evenly coated, then grill for 2 more minutes, or until golden brown.

Add the mussels to the wine pan and stir well. Put the lid on and cook for 4-5 minutes, or until all the mussels have opened. Discard any that haven't opened, then stir the 30g butter into the sauce and serve immediately with the wild garlic toasts.

name: my famous multipurpose dough

ingredients.raw: 1/2 cup (125ml) whole milk, heated to about 100°F (38°C)
1/2 cup (125ml) filtered water, heated to about 100°F (38°C)
3½ cups (525g) unbleached bread flour, plus more for dusting
ingredients.item: unbleached bread flour, plus more for dusting

directions: Make the tangzhong. In a small saucepan, whisk together the all-purpose flour, water, and milk until completely dissolved. Heat over medium heat, stirring continuously, until the mixture thickens into a thick paste. Transfer to a small bowl, and set aside to cool.
In a heatproof bowl, add the warmed milk and water. Stir in the yeast, cover with plastic wrap, and let sit for 10 minutes. The mixture should get lightly foamy, and the yeast should dissolve.

In the bowl of a stand mixer, whisk together the bread flour, sugar, and salt until thoroughly combined. Fit the stand mixer with the dough hook. Begin mixing on medium-low speed. One at a time, add the yeast mixture, the whole egg, and the egg yolk. Once the mixture starts to come together, add the tangzhong. Mix for 3 to 4 minutes, or until the dough is smooth and elastic.
Add the butter, and continue mixing on medium-low speed for another 2 minutes, or until all of the butter is incorporated and the dough is smooth and supple.
Transfer the dough to a large greased bowl, and cover with greased plastic wrap. Let rise at room temperature for 1½ hours, or until the dough has doubled in size.

Punch down the dough to release the gas and turn onto a lightly floured surface. Shape and bake as desired according to pages 62 to 63. Store the cooled buns or rolls in a resealable bag on the counter for up to 4 days or in an airtight container in the freezer for up to 2 months.

name: Rainbow Summer Rolls

ingredients.raw: 24 cooked and shelled sustainably sourced medium-sized prawns (175g)
ingredients.item: cooked and shelled sustainably sourced medium-sized prawns

ingredients.raw: 150g cucumber, deseeded, sliced into thin 4cm strips
1 small carrot (50g), sliced into thin 4cm strips
1 spring onion, trimmed and sliced into thin 4cm strips
a big handful of coriander leaves
ingredients.item: a big handful of coriander leaves

ingredients.raw: a big handful of mint leaves
ingredients.item: a big handful of mint leaves

ingredients.raw: 50g Vietnamese rice noodles, covered in boiling water for 3-4 minutes, then drained and rinsed in cold water
ingredients.item: Vietnamese rice noodles, covered in boiling water for 3-4 minutes, then drained and rinsed in cold water

directions: Stir together the dipping sauce ingredients, adding a dash of water if too salty.
Toss the prawns in the soy sauce/tamari and set aside. Prepare the noodles, all the vegetables and herbs and place within easy reach.
Fill a bowl, large enough to dunk the wrappers in, with lukewarm water.
When you're ready to wrap, gently place a rice paper wrapper into the water. Wait until it is just pliable, but not slippery and soft, only 1 minute or so, and remove from the water.
Lay it flat on a clean chopping board. Place 3 prawns in a line down the middle of the wrapper.
Arrange a little of each vegetable - be mean, not generous - in a line next to the prawns.
Top the prawns with a generous amount of coriander and 3-4 mint leaves.
Finish with a pinch of the softened noodles, arranged in a neat line next to the prawns.
Don't over-fill the delicate wrappers.
Fold the top and bottom of each wrapper down over the filling, then fold one side of the wrapper over to cover the filling.

Working from the folded side, gently turn the roll over on top of the remaining loose wrapper, quite tightly - this will help close the wrapper but also gently lifts it off the board, without sticking.
Place the roll on a serving plate and repeat until you've finished all 8.
Eat immediately, with the dipping sauce alongside.

name: Rib-eye Steaks with Peppercorn Sauce

ingredients.raw: 1 banana shallot, peeled and finely diced
1 large garlic clove, peeled and finely chopped
Sea salt and freshly ground black pepper
ingredients.item: Sea salt and freshly ground black pepper

directions: Place a large, non-stick frying pan over a high heat. Rub the olive oil over the steaks and season liberally with salt and pepper. When the pan is smoking hot, add the steaks and cook for 2-3 minutes on each side if you like your steaks medium rare.

Remove the pan from the heat, then add the thyme and butter, and baste, baste, baste the steaks for at least a minute. Turn them over and baste again. Transfer the steaks and thyme to a warm plate and leave to rest.
Return the pan to a medium heat, add the shallot and cook for 2-3 minutes, or until softened. Add the green peppercorns and garlic and cook for 1-2 minutes.

Pour in the cognac and flambé carefully. Add the mustard, beef stock and Worcestershire sauce, stir well and increase the heat to high. Let the stock reduce by half before adding the cream. Allow it to cook for another few minutes, or until the cream has thickened.

Put the rested steaks on serving plates, making sure to pour any resting juices into the sauce. Stir the sauce well and season to taste before pouring it over the steaks and serving with green vegetables.

name: ROAST BROCCOLI & BEANS WITH TAHINI SAUCE

ingredients.raw: 200g broccoli, ideally tenderstem or purple sprouting
ingredients.item: broccoli, ideally tenderstem or purple sprouting

ingredients.raw: 2 tablespoons tahini (look for a brand with minimal bitterness)
ingredients.notes: look for a brand with minimal bitterness

ingredients.raw: 2 teaspoons lemon juice, or to taste
1 clove of garlic, crushed to a paste
a pinch of salt, or to taste
ingredients.item: a pinch of salt, or to taste

directions: Heat the oven to 200°C/400°F/gas mark 6.

Tumble the beans and broccoli onto a large rimmed baking sheet, then add the oil and a pinch of salt. Toss, working the oil into the broccoli florets, then arrange in a single layer. Roast in the oven for 7-8 minutes, or until the broccoli heads are just beginning to char (don't let them burn, though).

Make the tahini sauce: mix together the tahini, lemon juice, garlic and salt. You will notice that, as you stir, the tahini thickens and darkens - this is fine, but you will need to add enough water to thin it to a pourable consistency. Do so, adding 1 tablespoon or so at a time. Taste, and add more salt or lemon, if needed.
When the vegetables are ready, transfer them to a warmed platter. Spread out, then drizzle with the tahini sauce and finally, sparingly, with the pomegranate molasses. Serve immediately.

name: Roast Pork Chops with Crushed Charlotte Potatoes and Lettuce and Apple Salad

ingredients.raw: Sea salt and freshly ground black pepper
ingredients.item: Sea salt and freshly ground black pepper

ingredients.raw: 2 spring onions, trimmed and finely sliced
1/2 dessert apple, peeled and shaved with a vegetable peeler

directions: Preheat the oven to 200°C/180°C fan/Gas 6.
Bring a kettle of water to the boil, then pour it into a saucepan. Season with salt and bring back to the boil over a medium-high heat. Add the potatoes and cook for 15 minutes, or until tender.

Place a large, ovenproof frying pan over a high heat. Drizzle the vegetable oil over the pork chops and season with salt and pepper. When the pan is hot, add the chops and cook for 1-2 minutes on each side. Add the butter and, when foaming, baste the meat with it. Add the garlic and thyme, then transfer the pan to the oven for 6-8 minutes. When the chops are cooked through, transfer to a warm plate and allow to rest.
Meanwhile, place a frying pan over a medium-high heat and add the bacon lardons. Cook for 5-8 minutes, or until crisp and golden. Drain on kitchen paper.
Combine the dressing ingredients in a bowl and whisk together.

When the potatoes are cooked, drain in a colander, then transfer to a bowl and crush lightly with a fork. Stir in the spring onions and crispy bacon. Drizzle over two-thirds of the dressing, then stir well and season with salt and pepper.
Combine the salad ingredients in a bowl, add the remaining dressing and toss well.
Drizzle the pork chops with some of the pan juices and serve with a generous spoonful of the crushed potatoes and the salad on the side.

name: RYE CRACKERS

ingredients.raw: 50g rye flour, plus extra for dusting
ingredients.item: rye flour, plus extra for dusting

directions: In a bowl, mix all the ingredients together, first with a spoon, and then using your hands to knead the dough into a neat ball. Wrap in clingfilm or cover with a clean, damp tea towel and set aside for about 1 hour.
When ready to cook, heat the oven to 200°C/400°F/gas mark 6. Line 2 or more baking sheets with baking paper.

Flour a clean work surface and rolling pin. Unwrap the dough and divide into 3 pieces. Roll out each piece until about 2mm thick, then cut into whatever shape you like-we go for triangles as they work well for dipping. Arrange the shapes on the baking sheets as you go.
Bake in the oven for 7 minutes, or until crisp.
Remove and allow to cool. Eat immediately. Alternatively, when completely cool, store in a sealed container and eat within 3 days.

name: Salmon Fish Finger Sandwich with Anchovy Mayo

ingredients.raw: 2 tablespoons extra virgin olive oil, plus more as needed
ingredients.item: extra virgin olive oil, plus more as needed

ingredients.raw: 400-450g wild sockeye salmon, skin removed, sliced into 3cm-wide fingers
salt and freshly ground black pepper
ingredients.item: salt and freshly ground black pepper

ingredients.raw: 4 anchovies (or more, to taste), finely chopped
8 slices toasted wholemeal bread or sourdough (can be WF/GF) or 4 warmed wholegrain wraps
ingredients.item: toasted wholemeal bread or sourdough or 4 warmed wholegrain wraps

directions: Mix all the ingredients for the mayo in a bowl until well combined. Set aside.

Beat 1 egg in a bowl (save the other - you may not need it) and stir in a splash of milk. Place a couple of tablespoons of flour into another bowl and season with salt and pepper. Tip the breadcrumbs into a third bowl.

Set a wide frying pan over a medium heat. When hot, cover the base with oil. Working briskly, dip the salmon into the flour, then the egg, letting the excess drain away, and then the breadcrumbs. Be sure every finger is well covered in each layer.

Fry each fish finger in the hot oil for about 2 minutes or until golden on the bottom, then turn gently, cooking each side briefly for maximum crunch. Repeat until all the fish fingers are cooked, adding more oil to the pan, or more coatings to the bowls, as needed. Keep the cooked fish fingers warm while you cook the rest.

Spread the bottom half of each sandwich with the anchovy mayo and place 3-4 fish fingers on top. Squeeze over a little lemon juice and finish with some lettuce, cucumber and the top slice of toast/bread. Alternatively, roll into wraps.

name: Scallops with Creamed Corn and Pancetta

ingredients.raw: Sea salt and freshly ground black pepper
ingredients.item: Sea salt and freshly ground black pepper

ingredients.raw: 2 garlic cloves, peeled and finely chopped

directions: Start by making the creamed corn. Place a small, non-stick frying pan over a medium heat. When hot, add the pancetta and cook for 2-3 minutes, or until the fat begins to render.
Meanwhile, place a saucepan over a medium-high heat and add the oil. When hot, add the onion and cook for 5 minutes, or until softened.

Remove the corn kernels from the cobs by standing each cob upright and running a sharp knife down the sides. Add a handful of the corn to the pancetta pan and cook for a further 2-3 minutes, or until the pancetta is crispy and brown.

Add the garlic to the onion and cook for 1-2 minutes, then add the paprika, double cream, thyme leaves, water and remaining corn. Stir well and cook over a medium heat for 10 minutes, or until the corn has softened and the sauce has thickened.
Meanwhile, cook the green beans in boiling salted water until tender. Drain and put to one side until needed.
When the corn has softened, stir through the Parmesan, sour cream and chopped parsley and remove from the heat.

Pour the olive oil over the scallops, season with salt and pepper and gently mix until well coated. Place a large, non-stick frying pan over a medium-high heat. When hot, carefully add the scallops and cook for 1-2 minutes on one side, then flip over, add the butter to the pan and cook for a further minute. Baste the scallops with the butter and remove the pan from heat.
Spoon the creamed corn into shallow bowls and add the green beans. Top with the scallops and sprinkle with the pancetta and corn mixture before serving.

name: Seared Beef & Grain Salad with Chilli, Coriander & Mint

ingredients.raw: 20g each of spelt grain, brown rice and buckwheat
ingredients.item: each of spelt grain, brown rice and buckwheat

ingredients.raw: 50g raw snap peas or mange tout, sliced into 2cm pieces
ingredients.item: raw snap peas or mange tout

ingredients.raw: 75g tenderstem broccoli, raw or blanched, stems cut into 5cm slices
ingredients.item: tenderstem broccoli, raw or blanched, stems cut into 5cm slices

ingredients.raw: leaves from 3 sprigs of mint, torn if large
a generous handful of coriander leaves
ingredients.item: a generous handful of coriander leaves

ingredients.raw: 1cm piece of ginger, peeled, finely grated

directions: Remove the steak from the fridge and leave to come up to room temperature.

Place the spelt grain and brown rice in a pan and cover with water. Bring to the boil, cover with a lid, reduce to a simmer and cook for 5 minutes. Add the buckwheat to the pan and cook for a further 15 minutes. Drain and tip onto a plate to cool.
Meanwhile, prepare all the salad vegetables and herbs and place everything in a large bowl. Stir together the dressing ingredients with 1 tablespoon of cold water in a small bowl.

Place a heavy-based frying pan over a high heat. When it is really hot, add a splash of cooking oil, swirling it around to coat the base. Add the steak and cook for 2 minutes until well browned, then turn and cook the other side (the steak should be quite rare). Remove from the pan and set aside to rest for 5 minutes.
Add the cooled grains to the salad bowl, then add half of the dressing and toss.
Slice the rested steak into 5mm-thick pieces, cutting against the grain of the meat. Dress the meat with a quarter of the remaining dressing.
Divide the salad between 2 plates, then top with the steak and peanuts. Serve with the remaining dressing on the side and eat immediately.

name: Skirt Steak with Chimichurri

ingredients.raw: 1kg skirt steak, trimmed of any excess fat and connective tissue, at room temperature
salt and freshly ground black pepper
ingredients.item: salt and freshly ground black pepper

directions: Make sure the steak has come up to room temperature – remove it from the fridge at least 30 minutes before cooking.
Heat the barbecue, grill pan or griddle pan until smoking hot.
Rub the steak with the olive oil and season generously with salt and pepper. Halve the steak if it's too big for your pan.

Place on the grill, or in the pan, and cook for 4-5 minutes on each side for medium-rare. Resist the temptation to touch it, and turn it only once. This will allow a beautiful, crunchy char to develop on each side.
Remove and place on a chopping board, cover with foil and leave to rest for 10 minutes.
Slice thinly, against the grain, and spoon over some chimichurri to serve.

name: smash burgers

ingredients.raw: 1 large sweet onion, sliced into thick rounds
1 lb (450g) ground beef (70-80% lean; double for %2 lb/225g burgers)
8 slices of cheese (such as Gouda, cheddar, or pepper jack)
ingredients.notes: such as Gouda, cheddar, or pepper jack

ingredients.raw: Kosher salt and freshly cracked black pepper, to taste
ingredients.item: Kosher salt and freshly cracked black pepper, to taste

directions: In a 10-inch (25-cm) skillet, heat the oil over medium-high heat. Once very hot and the oil begins to move around the pan, add the sliced onion. Season generously with salt. Cook, stirring often, until the onions are cooked and lightly charred. Set aside in a bowl to cool.
Prepare the mustard sauce. In a small bowl, mix together all of the ingredients. Cover with plastic wrap, and refrigerate until ready to use.

Portion the ground beef into 4 even balls (4 oz/110g each). Heat a large, dry cast-iron skillet over medium-high heat. Once the pan is hot, place the beef balls in the skillet, spacing them apart as much as you can, working in batches if needed. Smash them with a spatula to form flat patties about 1/2 inch (1.25cm) thick. Season the tops generously with salt to taste. Let the patties sear for about 3 minutes, or until you get a nice crust on the bottom. Flip the patties, season the other side with salt, and sear for another 3 to 4 minutes, or until the patties are fully cooked through and nicely browned on the bottom.

Add 2 slices of cheese on top of each patty in the pan. Add a small splash of water to the pan to create steam, and immediately cover with a lid or foil. Steam until the cheese is fully melted and then uncover and remove from the heat.
Spread the butter generously over the cut sides of the buns. In a large dry pan or on a griddle, toast all of the buns cut-side down over medium heat until beautifully browned.
Season the tomato slices on both sides with salt, and let them rest for 2 minutes.

Assemble the burgers. On the bottom buns, spread out a dollop of the mustard sauce, followed by a layer of sliced tomatoes, pickle slices, iceberg lettuce, and a beef patty. Top the patties with charred onions and another generous dollop of the sauce. Finally, place the top bun. Enjoy immediately.

name: SOUR THAI-STYLE SALAD

ingredients.raw: 1 red chilli, deseeded or not, finely chopped, or to taste
3 spring onions, sliced into small pieces on an angle
150g green beans, blanched, drained and cooled
ingredients.item: green beans, blanched, drained and cooled

ingredients.raw: 6cm piece of lemongrass, tough outer removed and discarded, very finely chopped
ingredients.item: piece of lemongrass, tough outer removed and discarded, very finely chopped

ingredients.raw: 2 teaspoons raw, unpasteurized apple cider vinegar (with mother)
juice of 1 lime, or more as needed
ingredients.item: juice of 1 lime, or more as needed

ingredients.raw: 1 tablespoon soy sauce/tamari (choose WF/GF if needed)

directions: Boil the kettle. Place the glass noodles into a heatproof bowl and cover with boiling water. Leave for 2 minutes, then drain and rinse in cold water. Set aside.
Put the cucumber, chilli, spring onions and green beans into a large serving bowl.
Toss well.
Stir together the dressing ingredients and taste - it should be salty, tangy and sour. Add a little more lime juice, if needed.

Just before serving, add the cooled noodles and the peanuts to the salad. Pour over half of the dressing and toss again. Taste, adding more dressing as you go - it should be flavourful, but don't make the salad wet. Serve immediately.

name: Soy & Ginger Braised Short Ribs

ingredients.raw: 2 tbsp peeled and grated fresh ginger

directions: Season the short ribs with salt and pepper and sear them on all sides in a large frying pan over a high heat with a little oil for a few minutes, then transfer to the slow cooker.

Add in all the remaining ingredients with 250ml of boiling water. Cook on high for 4 hours or low for 8 hours, or until the short ribs are completely tender. Turn the ribs around in the liquid a few times during cooking.
Once cooked, remove the short ribs to a serving platter and transfer the liquid to a saucepan. Bring to the boil and cook over a high heat until reduced to a glossy sauce consistency.
Drizzle the reduced sauce over the short ribs and serve with rice, some stir-fried pak choi, fresh coriander and sliced red chilli, if liked.

name: SPICY CHICKPEAS ON TOAST

ingredients.raw: 1-2 cloves of garlic, crushed, to taste
1-2 teaspoons finely chopped red chilli, or to taste
freshly squeezed lemon juice, to taste
ingredients.item: freshly squeezed lemon juice, to taste

ingredients.raw: salt and freshly ground black pepper, to taste
ingredients.item: salt and freshly ground black pepper, to taste

ingredients.raw: 2-4 slices of wholegrain sourdough, depending on their size
ingredients.item: wholegrain sourdough, depending on their size

ingredients.raw: butter or good-quality extra virgin olive oil, for spreading or brushing
ingredients.item: butter or good-quality extra virgin olive oil, for spreading or brushing

directions: Set a saucepan over a medium heat. When hot, add the oil and then the tomato purée and garlic, if using. Cook, stirring, for 1-2 minutes until the garlic stops smelling pungent and raw, and the oil and purée blend together.
Add the chickpeas, chilli and smoked paprika. Stir well and add the water, then cook for a further 2 minutes until everything is piping hot. Remove from the heat.
Toast the bread and butter it or brush with extra virgin olive oil.
Squeeze a little lemon juice into the chickpeas, mix and then taste. Add salt and pepper or more lemon juice, as needed. Pile the chickpeas onto the hot toast and crumble over the cheese.

name: SPICY SQUASH WITH CREAMY POLENTA

ingredients.raw: about 600g butternut squash, deseeded, skin on, cut into 3cm chunks
4 cloves of garlic, in their skins
a generous pinch of hot or mild chilli flakes, to taste
ingredients.item: a generous pinch of hot or mild chilli flakes, to taste

ingredients.raw: salt and freshly ground black pepper
ingredients.item: salt and freshly ground black pepper

ingredients.raw: extra virgin olive oil, to serve
ingredients.item: extra virgin olive oil, to serve

ingredients.raw: salt and freshly ground black pepper, to taste
ingredients.item: salt and freshly ground black pepper, to taste

directions: Heat the oven to 180°C/350°F/gas mark 4.

Use 1 large or 2 smaller roasting trays. Toss together the squash, onion, garlic, chilli flakes, if using, olive oil and some black pepper. Spread out in a single layer in the tray(s) and roast in the oven for 30 minutes.

Add the sliced chilli and toss again. Increase the heat to 200°C/400°F/gas mark 6 and cook for a further 25 minutes, or until the onion is browning and the squash tender (check after 15 minutes). Remove from the oven, take out the garlic and set aside. Season the roasted veg with a little salt and keep warm.

Meanwhile, cook the polenta. Bring the water and milk to a simmer in a deep pan. Pour in the polenta in a slow stream, stirring all the time to stop lumps forming. Reduce the heat and cook according to the packet instructions, stirring often. If the polenta gets too thick, add a little water or milk - it should be soft, creamy and not at all gritty. Season to taste with salt and pepper.

Pop the roasted garlic from their skins, mash to a paste with the flat side of a knife, then stir into the polenta. Serve the spicy squash and onions on top of the polenta, finishing with a little drizzle of extra virgin olive oil.

name: Spicy Thai Red Veggie Curry

ingredients.raw: 3 large sweet potatoes, peeled and chopped
2 tbsp light or dark soy sauce
1 tsp chilli flakes, plus extra if you like it spicy
ingredients.item: chilli flakes, plus extra if you like it spicy

ingredients.raw: 4 tbsp Thai red curry paste or 4 paste pots
ingredients.item: Thai red curry paste or 4 paste pots

ingredients.raw: thumb-sized piece of fresh ginger, grated
fresh coriander and grated lime zest, to garnish
ingredients.item: fresh coriander and grated lime zest, to garnish

ingredients.raw: 125g cooked rice per person and (optional) flatbreads, to serve
ingredients.item: cooked rice per person and flatbreads, to serve

directions: Add all the ingredients, except the sugar snap peas, garnishes and rice, to the slow cooker. Cook on high for 2-3 hours or low for 5-6 hours.
For the last hour of cooking, add the sugar snap peas so they still retain a slight crunch.
Top with fresh coriander and lime zest for some punch. Serve with rice and flatbreads to mop up that sauce, if you like.

name: SPINACH WITH RAISINS & PINE NUTS

ingredients.raw: 1 clove of garlic, very finely chopped
a knob of butter (optional, omit if DF/Ve)
ingredients.item: a knob of butter (optional, omit if DF/Ve)

ingredients.raw: a drizzle of extra virgin olive oil (optional)
ingredients.item: a drizzle of extra virgin olive oil (optional)

directions: Set a large lidded pan over a medium heat and add 2 tablespoons of water. Add the wet spinach, cover with the lid and cook for 1-2 minutes until just wilted but not completely cooked. Drain in a colander, using the back of a spoon to press out some of the excess liquid.

Set a large frying pan over a medium heat and add the olive oil. When hot, add the pine nuts and raisins and cook just until the pine nuts begin to turn golden, then add the garlic and cook for 1 minute longer. Next, add the butter and the spinach. Cook, stirring, until the spinach has completely wilted. If it releases a lot more liquid, you may want to cook it a little longer to let it bubble off-but don't pour it away as you will lose a lot of flavour.
Season with salt and finish with a drizzle of extra virgin olive oil, if you like. A squeeze of lemon brightens the flavours, too. Eat hot or warm.

name: STEAK BURRITO

ingredients.raw: salt and freshly ground black pepper
ingredients.item: salt and freshly ground black pepper

ingredients.raw: 100g cooked black beans, hot or warm
ingredients.item: cooked black beans, hot or warm

ingredients.raw: 4 tablespoons Guacamole, or 1 avocado, peeled, stoned and mashed (optional)
1/2 spring onion, sliced, or about 1 tablespoon Pink Pickled Onions, or both
a handful of fresh coriander leaves
ingredients.item: a handful of fresh coriander leaves

directions: Measure the rice in a mug, then work out what twice its volume of water would be and place both in a pan. Bring to the boil, then cover and cook for about 12 minutes, until tender. Remove from the heat, drain if necessary, and set aside (covered) while you prepare the rest of the fillings.
Mix together the cumin, oregano, cayenne pepper, garlic, lime juice and some salt and pepper in a small bowl. Set aside.

Season the steak all over. Pour a little oil into a frying pan set over a high heat. Once it's really hot, quickly cook the steak until deep brown on both sides but still fairly rare, just 2-3 minutes on each side. Remove from the pan and place in a bowl. Pour over the lime juice mixture and set aside to rest.

When ready to assemble, slice the steak into 1.5cm-thick strips. Arrange half the rice in a line on each wrap/flatbread, leaving space at the bottom to fold up and enclose the fillings later. Divide the black beans, feta, the steak strips and guacamole or avocado, if using, between the wraps. Arrange all the remaining vegetables and coriander leaves on top. Douse everything generously with hot sauce, then tightly wrap up each burrito, tucking up the spare wrap/flatbread at the bottom before rolling from the side, so that the filling doesn't fall out or drip. Dig in.

name: Steak Tacos with Pink Pickled Onion and Pico de Gallo

ingredients.raw: 6-8 x 15cm round Mexican corn or blue corn tortillas
ingredients.item: x 15cm round Mexican corn or blue corn tortillas

ingredients.raw: Sea salt and freshly ground black pepper
ingredients.item: Sea salt and freshly ground black pepper

ingredients.raw: 2 red onions, peeled and finely sliced
1 green jalapeño chilli, deseeded and sliced
Small handful of coriander, roughly chopped
1 ripe avocado, peeled, stoned and diced

directions: Sprinkle the steaks with the cumin and chilli powder. Drizzle with the olive oil and season with salt and pepper.
Put the sliced onions into a small bowl and cover with boiling water. Leave for 10 minutes.
Meanwhile, make the pico de gallo: put the tomatoes and jalapeño into a small bowl with the coriander, avocado and lime juice. Season to taste.
Drain the onions, then place them in a small bowl. Add the oregano, lime juice and a little salt and stir to combine.

Heat a large, non-stick frying pan over a high heat and cook the steaks for 3-4 minutes on each side. Transfer to a warm plate and leave to rest. Heat the tortillas in a large frying pan one at a time until lightly toasted on each side.
